description:
Fix CLI UX friction points identified during fresh-user QA testing. Includes: doctor command treating server-not-running as fatal instead of warning, opentabs with no args showing help instead of status, incomplete --show-secret help text, and doctor checking MCP client config in unreliable cwd-based paths.

LOW severity issues documented but not addressed: plugin search showing 'by unknown' author (recent fix 76cc5c68 may have addressed this), start --help not showing --show-config option description.

userStories.title: Downgrade doctor MCP server check from fatal to warning when server is not reachable
userStories.description: As a new user running opentabs doctor before starting the server, I want the MCP server check to show a warning instead of a fatal error, so that I understand the server just needs to be started rather than thinking something is broken.
acceptanceCriteria: When the MCP server is not reachable, checkServerHealth returns a warning (fatal: false) with hint 'Start it with: opentabs start', not a fatal error
doctor exits 0 when the only failing checks are the MCP server being unreachable and its dependent checks (extension connection, npm plugins)
doctor still exits 1 for genuinely fatal issues like an invalid config file
The summary message says 'Some warnings above may need attention' instead of 'Fix the errors above to proceed' when only warnings are present
Existing unit tests for doctor checks are updated to reflect the new severity
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts, lines 76-101. Change `fail('MCP server', 'not reachable', hint)` to `warn('MCP server', 'not reachable', hint)`. The `fail()` helper sets `fatal: true`, while `warn()` sets `fatal: false`. This one-line change makes the check a warning.

Also update checkExtensionConnected (line 104-116) — when server is not reachable, 'Extension connection: unknown (server not reachable)' should also remain a warning (it already uses `warn()`, so no change needed there).

Also update checkNpmPlugins (line 190-223) — 'npm plugins: requires running server to check' should remain a warning (already uses `warn()`).

The key behavior change: `hasFatal` at line 470 will now only be true for genuinely broken config, not for a server that hasn't been started yet. Test file: platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.test.ts — check if there are tests for checkServerHealth behavior.

userStories.title: Make opentabs with no args run handleStatus instead of showing help
userStories.description: As a user running opentabs with no arguments, I want to see the server status (or 'server not running' message) instead of the help text, so that the CLI behaves like a quick status check by default.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `opentabs` with no arguments invokes handleStatus (shows server status or 'Server not running' message)
Running `opentabs` with no arguments exits 0 when the server is running
Running `opentabs` with no arguments exits 1 when the server is not running (same as `opentabs status`)
Running `opentabs --help` still shows the full help text
Running `opentabs` with an unknown subcommand still shows the error with suggestion and help
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/cli.ts, line 35. The current code has `.action((_options, command: Command) => handleStatus(command.optsWithGlobals()))` which should make Commander call handleStatus when no subcommand is given. However, Commander's default behavior with subcommands is to show help when no args are provided.

The fix: check if Commander invokes the action. If it doesn't (shows help instead), the fix may require using Commander's `command('*')` hidden catch-all, or checking `process.argv.length` before parsing. The cleanest approach is probably to override Commander's default help display for no-args by checking if argv has no subcommand after parsing.

Alternative approach: In the program setup, do NOT call `.showHelpAfterError()` on the root program (only on subcommands), or use a pre-parse hook to detect no subcommand.

Test by running `node dist/cli.js` with no arguments — it should output status, not help.

userStories.title: Update --show-secret help text to mention MCP client config output
userStories.description: As a user reading help text for config show, I want to know that --show-secret also prints ready-to-copy MCP client configurations, so that I can discover this useful feature.
acceptanceCriteria: The --show-secret option description mentions both the secret and MCP client configs
The description reads something like 'Display the full authentication secret and MCP client configurations'
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, line 644. The current option definition is:
`.option('--show-secret', 'Display the full authentication secret')`

Change to:
`.option('--show-secret', 'Display the full authentication secret and MCP client configurations')`

This is a one-line text change. The behavior already works (commit e89d8f11 added MCP config printing to --show-secret), the help text just doesn't reflect it.

userStories.title: Improve doctor MCP client config check to use well-known home directory paths
userStories.description: As a user running opentabs doctor from any directory, I want the MCP client config check to find my Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenCode configurations regardless of my current working directory.
acceptanceCriteria: The Cursor MCP config check looks in ~/.cursor/mcp.json (home directory) in addition to the cwd-based .cursor/mcp.json
The OpenCode config check looks in common locations beyond just cwd/opencode.json
The check still passes when it finds a valid config in ANY of the checked locations
The hint message lists all checked paths so the user knows where to put the config
Existing unit tests for checkMcpClientConfig are updated
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts, lines 230-234. The `defaultMcpClientLocations` function currently checks:
- Claude Code: `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json` (correct — home-based)
- Cursor: `process.cwd()/.cursor/mcp.json` (unreliable — cwd-based)
- OpenCode: `process.cwd()/opencode.json` (unreliable — cwd-based)

For Cursor, add `~/.cursor/mcp.json` as an additional check path (Cursor stores global MCP config in the home directory). Keep the cwd-based path as a fallback for project-level configs.

For OpenCode, the config location is less standardized. Keep the cwd-based check but also consider common locations. If OpenCode doesn't have a well-known global config path, just keep cwd as-is and document it.

The `checkMcpClientConfig` function iterates through `clients` array and returns pass on the first match, so adding more paths is safe — it just checks more locations.

Update the warning hint message at line 263-267 to list the correct paths.
